_comment: Phase 30 (GOV-08, D-15/D-16) conservative service denylist + sensitive-origin seed. deniedOrigins: services where automation is categorically prohibited (rendered non-enableable by the consent gate, checked BEFORE per-origin policy). sensitiveOrigins: services that force extra friction (Ask / confirm) even under Auto but are not necessarily hard-denied. This is a CONSERVATIVE category seed, NOT an aggressive broad blocklist, and is USER-EXTENSIBLE. Host patterns use a leading 'https://*.' subdomain-wildcard form: 'https://*.chase.com' matches any subdomain of chase.com (and the bare apex); 'https://*.gov' matches any host ending in '.gov'. A pattern with no '*' matches that exact origin. See docs/LEGAL.md for the policy rationale. Phase 35 (v1.0.0, DENY-01/02) expands the roster ahead of OpenTabs descriptor import (opt-out Auto makes the denylist the ONE hard floor): brokerage/trading + ToS-hostile media/social are added to deniedOrigins; payments/budgeting + IG/FB/TikTok/X + messaging are added to sensitiveOrigins (reads under Auto, writes mutating-gated by posture B). Several roster origins use the EXACT-host form (digital.fidelity.com, dashboard.stripe.com, music.youtube.com NOT *.youtube.com, etc.) so a pattern is never broader than OpenTabs' own urlPattern host scope. Phase 38 (v1.0.0, BRDTH-02/03) screens the comms/social/content import batch ahead of plans 02/03: the AI-chat apps (chatgpt.com, claude.ai) and the microblog/fediverse social apps (bsky.app, mastodon.social, *.threads.net) are added to sensitiveOrigins as the CONSERVATIVE default for this category (reads run under Auto, writes are mutating-gated by posture B). They are classified EXPLICITLY here (fail-safe toward LESS reach); the classifyGate heuristic was ALSO widened (38-REVIEW MED-01) to independently flag this category on host alone, so the explicit line is no longer a single point of failure. discord.com is already sensitive (Phase 35). The DENY-01 ToS-hostile media/social set (netflix/spotify/twitch/steam/youtube-music/tinder/onlyfans) stays in deniedOrigins (never imported even DOM-only); reddit content-reads are SAFE (not added here -- reads run normally; the read-only-safe invariant is enforced by verify-catalog-crosscheck.mjs per 38-REVIEW MED-02). The chatgpt/claude/bsky/mastodon comms/social additions use the EXACT-host form (no '*.' broadening) so each pattern is no broader than the app's own origin; threads uses the '*.threads.net' subdomain-wildcard form (38-REVIEW LOW-01) so the screened set covers the bare apex + www + any subdomain (consistent with the other apex-bearing social brands), while the importer stays pinned to www.threads.net for slug derivation. Phase 39 (v1.0.0, BRDTH-01/02/03) screens the commerce/travel/misc import batch ahead of plans 02-06 per the PAYMENT / money-movement axis (the most-sensitive batch -- a payment op writable under Auto = money moved without consent): payment-bearing commerce/travel/paid-booking origins (food-delivery/rideshare doordash/ubereats/uber/lyft/grubhub/instacart/dominos/chipotle; retail/marketplace amazon/ebay/etsy/bestbuy/costco/walmart/target/craigslist/shopify; travel/transport booking/airbnb/expedia/kayak/opentable) are added to sensitiveOrigins as the CONSERVATIVE default for this category (reads run under Auto, WRITES posture-B mutating-gated by the existing _evaluateConsent). opentable is classified sensitive UNCONDITIONALLY (an OpenTable reservation holds a card -- a held-card / paid reservation is payment-adjacent). The most catastrophic standalone money-movement / pure-payment apps (paypal/venmo/cashapp/wise/western-union) are added to deniedOrigins (like DENY-01: never imported, even DOM-only) -- their primary function is direct money movement with no benign read surface. Genuinely read-only commerce browsing / availability apps (calendly/yelp/tripadvisor/zillow/grafana) are left UNCLASSIFIED (safe): their vendored ops are all reads, and Task-2's widened finance/payment heuristic uses deliberately SPECIFIC tokens (checkout/cart/place-order/charge, NOT a bare 'order'/'book'/'reserve') so a calendly availability read or a yelp business search does NOT false-trip. A safe commerce origin is added to verify-catalog-crosscheck.mjs READ_ONLY_SAFE_SERVICES ONLY by its own import plan and ONLY when its emitted ops are verified read-only (the 38 MED-02 guard). The commerce/travel additions use EXACT-host (www.<app>) or apex-suffix ('https://*.uber.com'/'https://*.lyft.com'/'https://*.shopify.com'/'https://*.venmo.com') forms, each pattern no broader than the app's own origin. The DENY-01 set + the Phase-35 banking/payments seed + the Phase-38 comms/social additions all stay UNCHANGED. Phase 39-05 (v1.0.0, BRDTH-01/02/03) extends this roster for the events sub-batch ahead of its import: the PAYMENT-bearing ticket apps (ticketmaster/stubhub -> buy tickets; eventbrite -> register for a paid event = money movement) are added to sensitiveOrigins (EXACT-host www.<app> form) -- reads run under Auto, the buy_tickets/register_for_event WRITES posture-B mutating-gated. The READ-ONLY local-services/scheduling apps in the SAME sub-batch (yelp/tripadvisor reviews+listings; calendly availability) are left UNCLASSIFIED (safe): their vendored ops are all reads (search/get/list -- no payment verb), the 39-01-widened heuristic's SPECIFIC tokens do NOT false-trip them, and each is added to verify-catalog-crosscheck.mjs READ_ONLY_SAFE_SERVICES by THIS plan ONLY because its emitted ops are verified read-only (the 38 MED-02 guard -- a future write on one then FAILS the build). The earlier roster (incl. the 39-04 travel/transport additions) stays UNCHANGED.
deniedReason: Automation prohibited for this service category (financial / government). See docs/LEGAL.md.
